core_model.structure_summary: Each of several agents maximizes a payoff that is bilinear in a continuous allocation vector constrained to the simplex and a binary activation vector indicating which allocation components are positive, minus a linear cost weighted by the activations, with best responses characterized by first-order complementary-slackness conditions and an equilibrium in which adding congestion costs makes every channel congested.
core_model.canonical_form: \max_{x, v} \; \Pi = \sum_{j} (b_j - c_j)\, v_j x_j \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_j x_j = 1, \; x_j \ge 0, \; v_j = \mathbf{1}[x_j > 0]; \quad \mathcal{L} = \sum_j (b_j - c_j) x_j + \mu\left(1 - \sum_j x_j\right)

variables.meaning: continuous allocation fractions across links, summing to one
binary indicator variables switched on exactly when the paired continuous variable is positive
per-link cost coefficients, each strictly below its paired benefit
Lagrange multipliers enforcing the simplex constraint and inequality constraints
net payoff: benefit minus cost, bilinear in allocation and activation

notes: The structure is a multi-agent constrained maximization on hypercube-shaped graphs of increasing dimension, analyzed via Kuhn-Tucker/Lagrangian conditions; the headline result is an equilibrium property (all channels used, hence all congested once congestion costs are added). The extracted equations are notationally idiosyncratic and the congestion-cost extension itself is not shown explicitly, so the description of that extension relies on the abstract; classification between game_theoretic and constrained optimization is soft since the game reduces to independent per-agent optimizations.
